classes.name: AgentToolUseTracker
attributes: agent_map: dict[str, set[str]]  # name-keyed, for serialization
agent_to_tools: list[tuple[Agent, list[str]]]  # instance-keyed, for runtime

key_functions: _execute_tool_plan(plan, bindings, hooks, context_wrapper, run_config, parallel) -> tuple[...8 lists...]  # in tool_planning; dispatches all execute_xxx in parallel or serial
execute_function_tool_calls(bindings, tool_runs, hooks, context_wrapper, config, isolate_parallel_failures) -> tuple[FunctionToolResults, InputGuardrailResults, OutputGuardrailResults]
execute_computer_actions(public_agent, actions, hooks, context_wrapper, config) -> list[RunItem]
execute_custom_tool_calls(public_agent, calls, hooks, context_wrapper, config) -> list[RunItem]
execute_shell_calls(public_agent, calls, hooks, context_wrapper, config) -> list[RunItem]
execute_apply_patch_calls(public_agent, calls, hooks, context_wrapper, config) -> list[RunItem]
execute_local_shell_calls(public_agent, calls, hooks, context_wrapper, config) -> list[RunItem]
resolve_approval_status(tool_name, call_id, raw_item, agent, context_wrapper, on_approval) -> tuple[bool|None, ToolApprovalItem]
append_approval_error_output(generated_items, agent, tool_call, tool_name, call_id, message) -> None  # approvals.py
process_hosted_mcp_approvals(original_pre_step_items, mcp_approval_requests, context_wrapper, agent, append_item) -> tuple[list[ToolApprovalItem], set[str]]
key_relationships: ToolExecutionPlan aggregates ToolRunFunction, ToolRunComputerAction, ToolRunCustom, ToolRunShellCall, ToolRunApplyPatchCall, ToolRunLocalShellCall, ToolApprovalItem
_FunctionToolBatchExecutor depends_on RunContextWrapper (approval checks, tool state scope)

_execute_tool_plan calls execute_function_tool_calls, execute_computer_actions, execute_custom_tool_calls, execute_shell_calls, execute_apply_patch_calls, execute_local_shell_calls (parallel via asyncio.gather)
ComputerAction/ShellAction/CustomToolAction/ApplyPatchAction/LocalShellAction all call resolve_approval_status and produce ToolCallOutputItem
AgentToolUseTracker used by maybe_reset_tool_choice to decide whether to reset ModelSettings.tool_choice
approvals.py helpers (append_approval_error_output, filter_tool_approvals) consumed by run loop to build model input items
ToolApprovalItem is the pending-approval placeholder returned instead of ToolCallOutputItem when approval is needed
approval_flow: 1. Tool run evaluated by function_needs_approval() / evaluate_needs_approval_setting()
2. If needs approval: resolve_approval_status() called → queries RunContextWrapper.get_approval_status()
3a. approval_status=None (pending): ToolApprovalItem returned as run_item (interrupts run loop)
3c. approval_status=False: resolve_approval_rejection_message() called → rejection ToolCallOutputItem returned
4. on_approval callback (if set) can call context_wrapper.approve_tool() / reject_tool() to set status
5. MCP approvals have two paths: on_approval_request callback (async) or manual approval via human-in-the-loop
guardrail_integration: Results collected in tool_input_guardrail_results / tool_output_guardrail_results on executor
batch_execution_lifecycle: 2. execute(): resolve_enabled_function_tools() → create asyncio.Task per tool run
4. On failure: _raise_failure_after_draining_siblings() → cancel siblings, drain cancelled tasks, wait post_invoke tasks, merge failures, raise
5. _run_single_tool(): check approval → run input guardrails → fire on_tool_start hooks → invoke tool → run output guardrails → fire on_tool_end hooks
6. _build_function_tool_results(): assemble FunctionToolResult with nested agent run result and interruptions
important_notes: tool_execution.py hosts execution helpers; tool_actions.py hosts XXXAction classes (ComputerAction, ShellAction, etc.)
tool_planning.py hosts ToolExecutionPlan, plan builders, _execute_tool_plan dispatcher
approvals.py provides approval item manipulation utilities for the run loop
tool_use_tracker.py tracks per-agent tool usage to support model_settings.tool_choice auto-reset
All Action.execute() classmethods use with_tool_function_span() wrapper for tracing
Parallel execution: function tools batch in _FunctionToolBatchExecutor; other tool types run serially within asyncio.gather across categories
isolate_parallel_failures=True when multiple function tools are running concurrently
ToolApprovalItem is used as a pending placeholder; MCPApprovalResponseItem signals a completed MCP approval
